$schema_note: Canonical structured representation of the Eduversal School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (2026). All `verbatim` fields preserve original document wording. This JSON is the single source of truth for org chart, reports-to topology, meeting ecosystem, and governance flow definitions across the Eduversal network. Edit JSON directly; do NOT seed Firestore docs as the source of truth.

document.title: School Organizational & Meeting Framework
document.subtitle: Governance, Reporting Lines & Meeting Ecosystem
document.tagline: Success is the sum of details

designed_for: System-wide governance alignment across all Eduversal partner schools
Role clarity, accountability, and decision authority at every level
Integration with the Eduversal School Appraisal Handbook
Transferability to new partner schools during onboarding and consultancy engagements

non_negotiable_reporting_rules.preamble_verbatim: The following rules are mandatory across all Eduversal schools and cannot be modified at the school level:

rules.verbatim: Principals and Guidance Counselors report directly to the Foundation Representative / General Manager (FR/GM). They stand at the same hierarchical level.
Each educational division (KG, Primary, Middle, High) maintains an identical structure for consistency and scalability.
All official reporting, documentation, and signing authorities follow the defined hierarchy strictly.
Support services (Marketing, Finance, Facility, Alumni) report directly to FR/GM; Parent Coordinator and College Counselor report to the Guidance Coordinator.

principles.name: Divisional symmetry
principles.operational_meaning_verbatim: Every division (KG-HS) replicates the same structural template — simplifies Foundation oversight and partner school onboarding.

principles.name: Dual leadership lines
principles.operational_meaning_verbatim: Instructional leadership (Principal) and Guidance leadership (Guidance Counselor) operate in parallel — both feed into FR/GM and both participate in the SLT.

principles.name: One meeting, one owner
principles.operational_meaning_verbatim: Every meeting has a single Chair who owns the agenda, minutes, and follow-up — no duplicate forums.

principles.name: Evidence-driven
principles.operational_meaning_verbatim: Every recurring meeting generates documented outputs that feed the School Appraisal System (EDUNAV).

principles.name: Foundation-level advisory
principles.operational_meaning_verbatim: Eduversal Heads of Departments monitor and support; they do not chair school-level operational meetings.

organizational_chart.description_verbatim: The chart below is the standardized structure replicated across every Eduversal school. Partner schools adapt staffing numbers but not the reporting topology.
organizational_chart.legend_verbatim: Principal leads the Instructional line; Guidance leads the Welfare & Counselling line. Both report directly to FR/GM. At each divisional level they coordinate — but neither supervises the other.

support_services.verbatim_note: Support Services (Marketing, Finance, Facility, Alumni) report directly to FR/GM. Parent Coordinator and College Counselor report to the Guidance Coordinator.

roles_reporting_lines.description_verbatim: The following table is the authoritative reference for reporting relationships. Partner schools must reflect these lines in their internal documentation and org chart.

rows.direct_reports_or_working_relationship: All Principals, All Guidance Counselors, Marketing Department, Finance, Facility Manager, Alumni Coordinator
Academic Coordinators, School Subject Leaders, Cambridge Exam Officer, Olympiad Coord., Project Coord., Club Coord., Discipline Coord., School Culture Coord., Subject Teachers
Class Teachers, OSIS Coordinator, Dormitory Manager & Tutors, Parent Coordinator, College Counselor
Coordinates curriculum delivery and cross-subject planning within the division
Department teachers; department meetings; appraisal evidence collection
Exam registration, compliance, results analysis; coordinates with Eduversal Cambridge Unit
Olympiad programs; coordinates with Eduversal Head of Olympiads / Competitions
Project-based learning programs, student projects, inter-disciplinary initiatives
Extracurricular clubs, activity calendar, club supervisors
Student conduct standards, attendance, disciplinary actions; coordinates with Guidance on welfare cases
School values and culture programs; coordinates with Guidance on student-facing initiatives
Student welfare, parent contact, class-level welfare data
Student body activities, leadership development
Boarding operations, dormitory tutors, welfare
Parent engagement, parent communications, PTA-equivalent forums
University placement; supports HS Principal on academic readiness; aligned with Eduversal Head of College Counseling
Marketing, communications, content; coordinates with Eduversal Marketing and Principals
Budgeting, accounting, financial reporting to Foundation
Infrastructure, security, kitchen, cleaning, maintenance
Alumni-University and Alumni-Working networks; coordinates with Eduversal Head of Alumni Relations

meeting_ecosystem.description_verbatim: The meeting ecosystem is built in five functional tracks. Every recurring meeting has a single accountable Chair, a fixed cadence, and mandatory documented outputs. Meetings feeding the School Appraisal System are tagged with the relevant Domain (D1-D5).

appraisal_domain_legend.D1: School Development (mission, SMART goals, PIE model)

meetings.name: Senior Leadership Management Meeting
meetings.participants: Principals, Guidance Counselors, Marketing, Facility Manager
meetings.key_outputs: Weekly decisions, action log, cross-division alignment

meetings.name: Strategic Planning Meeting
meetings.key_outputs: Strategic priorities, SMART goals, PIE action plans

meetings.name: School Appraisal & Performance Review
meetings.key_outputs: Appraisal findings, improvement plan, KPI review

meetings.name: Principal - Academic Coordinators Meeting
meetings.participants: Academic Coord., Olympiad Coord., Project Coord., Career Planner

meetings.name: Academic General (Teachers) Meeting
meetings.participants: Principal + All Teachers (separate for KG / Primary / Secondary)

meetings.name: Departmental Meetings
meetings.key_outputs: Student outcome analysis, assessment & pedagogy actions

meetings.name: Guidance Coordination Meeting
meetings.participants: All Guidance Counselors, Dormitory Manager, OSIS Coord., Parent Coord., College Counselor
meetings.key_outputs: Welfare reports, dormitory status, safeguarding log, parent & placement updates

meetings.name: Safeguarding / Child Protection Review
meetings.participants: Guidance Counselors + Principal (by invite) + designated Safeguarding Lead
meetings.key_outputs: Case log, escalation decisions, policy compliance

meetings.name: University Placement Meeting
meetings.participants: HS Guidance, Career Planner, Alumni-University Coord.
meetings.key_outputs: Final-year application pipeline, readiness benchmarks, placement tracking

standard_weekly_schedule.description_verbatim: This is the standardized weekly rhythm for every Eduversal school. Times are indicative but the structure is mandatory. Partner schools synchronize to this rhythm during onboarding.

grid.meeting: Academic General Meeting (Teachers) — Separate: KG / Primary / Secondary

notes_verbatim: Academic General Meeting is mandatory for all teaching staff.
Principal - School Subject Leaders and Departmental meetings alternate bi-weekly.
Marketing Coordination, Alumni Coordination, and ad-hoc meetings are scheduled around the fixed blocks above.
Safeguarding, Appraisal Review, and Strategic Planning meetings are scheduled monthly / termly outside this weekly grid.

governance_decision_flow.description_verbatim: The table below shows how decisions flow through the meeting ecosystem. Each row is a decision category, its originating forum, and the authority empowered to finalize it. This prevents duplication and clarifies ownership.

decisions.originating_forum: Department Meeting -> Principal - School Subject Leaders
Classroom observation + School Subject Leader input -> Appraisal Conference
decisions.final_authority: Principal with Eduversal Head of Appraisal calibration

decisions.originating_forum: Guidance - Class Teachers -> Guidance Coordination -> Safeguarding Review
decisions.final_authority: FR/GM on recommendation of Guidance Coordinator
Guidance Coordinator + College Counselor (HS Principal consulted)
FR/GM with Eduversal Head of Marketing approval

decisions.originating_forum: Class Teacher -> Guidance -> Discipline Coordinator -> Principal (joint review)
decisions.final_authority: Principal for conduct sanctions; Guidance for welfare response; FR/GM for serious cases

principle_of_dual_authority.verbatim: For any matter that touches both instruction and wellbeing, Principal and Guidance co-decide through a documented joint review. Neither party can override the other; escalation goes to FR/GM.

appraisal_handbook_alignment.description_verbatim: Every recurring meeting in this framework is designed to generate evidence required by the Eduversal School Appraisal System. The mapping below is the official reference used by Eduversal Heads of Departments during appraisal visits.

domains.title: School Development
domains.feeding_meetings: Strategic Planning Meeting; SLT Management Meeting; Alumni Coordination
domains.evidence_generated: School development goals; SMART goal documents; PIE action plans; Leadership Team list; Job descriptions

domains.title: School Management & Leadership
domains.feeding_meetings: SLT Management Meeting; Guidance Coordination; Safeguarding Review
domains.evidence_generated: Organizational Chart; Staff Handbook; Parent-Student Handbook; Meeting minutes; Safeguarding logs

domains.title: Subject Departments & PD
domains.feeding_meetings: Principal - School Subject Leaders Meeting; Departmental Meetings
domains.evidence_generated: Department information sheet; Bi-weekly department minutes; School Subject Leader coordination minutes; PD self-evaluations & goals

domains.title: Olympiad & EdTech
domains.feeding_meetings: Principal - Academic Coordinators; Olympiad Coordinator reporting line
domains.evidence_generated: Olympiad participation & results log; EdTech integration evidence

domains.title: Curriculum, Teaching & Assessment
domains.feeding_meetings: Academic General Meeting; Departmental Meetings; Appraisal Conferences
domains.evidence_generated: Curriculum maps; Lesson observation rubrics (4 domains); Assessment data; Student work samples

rules: Agenda submitted 24 hours before every meeting.
Standardized minutes template; minutes distributed within 24 hours of the meeting.
Action items logged with owner, deadline, and status.
All documentation maintained in EDUNAV for Foundation review.
Department meeting minutes must include student outcome analysis — not only administrative updates.

partner_school_transferability.description_verbatim: This framework is designed to be installed in any new Eduversal partner school. The matrix below separates what is standardized (non-negotiable) from what is customizable.

matrix.rule_for_partner_school_verbatim: NON-NEGOTIABLE. Principal & Guidance report to FR/GM at the same hierarchical level.
NON-NEGOTIABLE. Identical replicated template regardless of school size.
NON-NEGOTIABLE structure; days/times adjustable to local context.
NON-NEGOTIABLE. All minutes, action items, and evidence logged centrally.
Local naming allowed (e.g., Kepala Sekolah, Wali Kelas) as long as reporting line is preserved.
Customizable to school size; minimum 1 Principal + 1 Guidance per active division.
Scope customizable (e.g., smaller schools may combine Finance + Admin) but all functions must be covered.

items.objective: Standardize governance across all Eduversal schools
items.key_actions: Deploy this document as the single reference; lock reporting topology; audit partner schools against it annually
items.responsibility: Eduversal Heads of Departments / FR-GM / Partner School FR
items.expected_outcome: Consistent structure, faster onboarding, predictable appraisal outcomes
items.risk_gap: Inconsistent adoption if partner FR resists — requires Eduversal audit follow-through

items.key_actions: Maintain Guidance Counselor's direct reporting line to FR/GM; include Guidance in SLT by default
items.expected_outcome: Safeguarding integrity, reduced conflict of interest
items.risk_gap: Occasional ambiguity on joint matters — mitigate with documented joint-decision protocol

items.objective: Link meetings directly to appraisal evidence
items.key_actions: Tag every recurring meeting with Appraisal Domain; enforce EDUNAV minutes
items.responsibility: Principal / School Subject Leader / Guidance / Eduversal Head of Appraisal
items.expected_outcome: Evidence always ready; appraisal becomes a byproduct of normal operations
items.risk_gap: Documentation fatigue if templates are too heavy — keep templates lean

items.objective: Make the framework replicable in new schools
items.key_actions: Standardized onboarding; Eduversal Head of Departments shadows the first full cycle
items.responsibility: Eduversal Heads of Departments / Marketing / Partner FR
items.expected_outcome: New partner schools operational within a standard onboarding cycle

items.key_actions: Eduversal Heads of Departments design templates & calendars; schools execute and report — no double-coordination meetings
items.expected_outcome: Principals focus on instructional leadership, not admin
items.risk_gap: Scope creep — monitor quarterly to ensure Eduversal stays advisory

downstream_consumers.$comment: Files / collections / pages that should read from this JSON. Update when new consumers are added.

current.uses: reportsTo[] arrays per Framework section 3-4 mapping table
Org Chart tab renders dual-line topology; description text references Framework v1.0

candidates: Future meeting-tracker module reading meeting_ecosystem + standard_weekly_schedule
Future EDUNAV evidence pipeline reading appraisal_handbook_alignment.domains[]
